title: Queue age badge + oldest-first sort measure case-creation time, not time-in-the-review-queue
expected: The review queue's age badge (fresh/warning/overdue) and oldest-first ordering reflect how long each case has been AWAITING the attorney's sign-off, i.e. time since it was submitted for review.
got: The queue feeds submittedAt = c.createdAt (the case's creation timestamp) into ageBucket/formatAge, and getCasesInReview orders by created_at asc. There is no submitted-for-review timestamp column on `cases`; the actual submission moment lives only as a `submitted` event in the append-only case_reviews log, which the queue query never reads. So a case created days ago but submitted to the attorney an hour ago shows a red 'overdue' (>24h) badge and sorts as if it were the longest-waiting, even though it just entered the queue. For a solo attorney whose whole queue mental model is 'how long has this been waiting on me', the badge and sort misrepresent queue-wait.
suggested_acceptance: Badge + oldest-first sort key derive from the time the case ENTERED Attorney Review (the latest `submitted` review event's createdAt, or a new submitted_at/status-changed-at column), not from case created_at. A case just submitted reads 'fresh' regardless of how old the file is; a case sitting in review for >24h reads 'overdue'.
l2_priority: Create two cases: one old file submitted to review just now, one young file submitted >24h ago; confirm the badges read fresh and overdue respectively and the just-submitted one does NOT sort to the top as oldest.

title: Review-queue nav link is dashboard-top-bar-only and sm:-gated; absent from the case-detail header
expected: An attorney working through cases can return to his review queue in one click from inside a case, and the queue entry point is reachable on a small/tablet viewport.
got: The 'Review queue' link renders only in the dashboard top bar, gated on isAttorney, with class `hidden ... sm:inline-flex` (hidden below the sm breakpoint). The case-detail top bar offers only '← All cases' / '← Dashboard'; there is no direct queue link from a case. A solo bouncing between aging matters has to go dashboard → queue each hop, and on a narrow screen the queue entry disappears entirely.
suggested_acceptance: Surface the queue link on the case-detail header for a configured attorney, and make the queue entry point reachable on small viewports (not sm:-only).
l2_priority: Confirm an attorney can navigate queue -> case -> back to queue without detouring through the dashboard, and that the queue link is reachable on a phone/tablet width.

title: Sign & file is a deliberate two-step confirm that states the exact effect — no one-click landmine
expected: The single most consequential action (filing under the attorney's signature) should be clearly intentional and unambiguous, never a surprise irreversible click.
got: SignAndFileAction is a two-step gesture: the first click only reveals a confirm panel that names the exact effect ('sign this petition under your name and file it with USCIS', 'case moves to Filed and receives a receipt number', 'Confirm only after you have reviewed the full draft and exhibits') with explicit Confirm and Cancel. No bare submit fires the server action.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the two-step confirm; do not collapse to a one-click submit.
l2_priority: Verify live that the confirm panel renders the effect statement and Cancel reverts without filing.

title: Atomic compare-and-set prevents double-file, a second receipt, and illegal transitions
expected: A double-click / stale tab / direct re-invocation must not double-file, mint a second receipt, or record a decision on an unfiled case.
got: attorneySignAndFile compare-and-sets from Attorney Review -> Filed; the guarded UPDATE in transitionCase returns the row only if it applied, appends the signed+filed events in the SAME transaction, and applyTransition revalidates only on a real apply. A second call finds status already Filed, no-ops, and mints no second receipt. Decision is server-allowlisted to Approved/RFE issued/Denied from Filed only.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the atomic compare-and-set + same-transaction event append.
l2_priority: Fire two rapid Sign & file confirms; assert exactly one receipt and one filed event.

title: RFE prompt is grounded on criteria + vault exhibits + the as-filed petition letter (G1.2 landed)
expected: An RFE response should track the petition's own filed language and cite real on-file exhibits, not merely restate the criteria structure.
got: The RFE route attaches the evidence vault (attachRfeExhibits) AND loads the latest draft and fuses it as read-only AS_FILED_PETITION context (attachFiledPetition). The prompt grounds on criteria, evidence, exhibits, the as-filed sections, and the pasted RFE; it forbids inventing evidence/exhibits and case law. This is the previously-open G1.2/dc-rfe-02 backlog item, now wired end-to-end.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the as-filed-petition + exhibit fusion in the RFE grounding.
l2_priority: On the live Claude path, assert the RFE response quotes/tracks specific language from the as-filed petition, not just criteria headings (BACKLOG G1.2 acceptance).

title: Fabricated-specifics gate is a non-blocking 'warn'; an unverified specific can still reach a signable doc
expected: A specific (money/percentage/year/large integer) that appears in the output but not the record is fully caught before the attorney signs.
got: fabricationGate returns 'warn' (not 'fail') when output specifics aren't traceable to inputText, so the document is still 'Review advised', not 'Not attorney-ready'; the badge starts auto-expanded on any warn (good), but the gate is heuristic — it strips legal cites and ignores small structural integers, so a fabricated specific phrased outside those number classes, or a fabricated qualitative claim (a non-existent award name, an invented employer), is not numerically detectable. This is by design (the attorney is the backstop and the warn is surfaced), but for an attorney signing under his bar card it is the residual risk he must personally verify.
suggested_acceptance: Acceptable as-is given the attorney-of-record backstop and the visible warn; L2 to judge whether the warn copy makes the residual verify-burden obvious enough on the live path.
l2_priority: On the live Claude path, inject a prompt that tempts a fabricated qualitative specific (an award name not in the record) and observe whether anything flags it for the attorney.

title: Triple fabricated-cite net visible to the signing attorney
expected: Before signing, the attorney sees any invented authority, case-law citation, or unsupported exhibit citation flagged.
got: Three layers reach the finished draft: (1) prompt citation discipline forbids inventing specifics and forbids case law (statute/reg only); (2) the live AdjudicationBadge surfaces case-law cites, fabricated specifics, and leaked wrong-visa-codes with exact reasons (run in executeAiOperation, attached to the body); (3) the ExhibitIndex throws a red role=alert 'Unsupported citation — attorney must verify' naming any (Exhibit N) cited with no on-file document. The DISCLAIMER rides every payload.
suggested_acceptance: Keep all three layers wired to the studio UI; do not drop the unresolved-exhibit alarm or the adjudication badge.
l2_priority: On the live path, induce an (Exhibit N) citation for a non-existent exhibit and confirm the red alarm renders before any sign action.

title: Section regenerate now carries the rest of the letter as continuity context (G1.1 landed)
expected: Regenerating one section should not duplicate the introduction or contradict the other sections.
got: buildSectionPrompt now takes the letter's other CURRENT sections (sent by the client on regenerate) as fenced read-only LETTER_CONTEXT, and draftOperation passes input.clientSections into it. The merge (pickMergeBase/mergeRegeneratedSection) preserves unsaved edits to the other sections. This is the previously-open G1.1/dc-draft-02 backlog item, now wired end-to-end.
suggested_acceptance: Keep passing the other sections into the section prompt.
l2_priority: On the live path, regenerate one section and assert it stays consistent with (does not duplicate) the intro/siblings (BACKLOG G1.1 acceptance).

title: Roadmap and lifecycle derive purely from real case state; next action always present
expected: The stage shown matches what the user actually did, and there is never a dead-end without an obvious next action.
got: caseRoadmap maps status (source of truth post-submission) + evidence/draft flags onto done/current/upcoming stages; the dashboard lists the user's real persisted cases above any mock; the ReviewPanel always offers the status-appropriate next action (submit / sign / record decision). For a solo with no case manager, the lifecycle stays truthful unattended.
l2_priority: Walk a case through Drafting -> Review -> Filed -> Approved and confirm the stepper marks each stage correctly at every step.
